metadata.seed: I hate looking at myself in the mirror, every time I do I feel like the ugliest person in the whole world. I feel like nobody will ever love me.

nobody will ever love me

patient.query: I mean, I see what you’re saying, but it’s just so hard to believe anything different sometimes. It’s like those thoughts are on repeat in my head, and I don’t know how to change the channel. I want to feel better, but I can’t help but think I’ll just keep feeling this way.

patient.query: I mean, I guess I’m open to it, but I’m just not sure if it’ll really help. Sometimes it feels like those thoughts take over, and I don’t know how to push back against them. I just want to feel normal, whatever that is.

Your thoughts about the therapy seem to be mixed with uncertainty and anxiety. It's understandable to feel this way, especially when dealing with thoughts that feel overwhelming. Let's explore together how we can work on finding ways to manage these thoughts and help you feel more in control.

patient.query: Yeah, I know it makes sense that I’d feel uncertain. It’s just really hard to imagine being in control when those thoughts are so loud. Sometimes it feels like I’m just going through the motions, and I can’t see a way out of it. I want to feel like myself again, but I just don’t know how to get there.

patient.query: I guess when I look in the mirror, I often think things like, “I look terrible,” or “People are judging me.” Those thoughts just flood in, and I can’t seem to push them away. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop, and I don’t even know where it started.

automatic_thoughts: I look terrible
People are judging me

REASONING:

It's important to understand that these thoughts might not always reflect reality and can often be influenced by deeper beliefs or assumptions. Let's work on challenging these thoughts and finding more balanced ways of thinking about yourself.

patient.query: I appreciate that, but it’s difficult to believe there’s a different reality when I feel so strong about my appearance. It’s almost like no matter what evidence I have, those thoughts just drown it all out. I guess I just want to feel like I matter and not just be defined by how I look.

automatic_thoughts: there's a different reality
those thoughts drown it all out
I just want to feel like I matter
I'm defined by how I look

patient.query: It’s really hard to see any other reality when those thoughts are so persistent. It feels like they just take over everything, and no matter what anyone says, I still end up feeling like I don’t matter unless I look a certain way. I just wish I could see myself in a better light, but that seems so far away right now.

I wish I could see myself in a better light
